title: Pomatocalpa armigerum
taxonomy: Pomatocalpa armigerum is a species of orchid in the genus Pomatocalpa. It belongs to the family Orchidaceae. Within the Orchidaceae family, it falls under the subfamily Epidendroideae. The subfamily Epidendroideae is further divided into several tribes, and Pomatocalpa armigerum is part of the tribe Cranichideae. This classification helps in understanding the evolutionary relationships and characteristics of the plant.

intro: Pomatocalpa armigerum is a species of orchid native to the tropical regions of Southeast Asia. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which provide relief from pain and promote wound healing. The bioactive constituents of Pomatocalpa armigerum include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used in herbal preparations, such as decoctions and infusions, and is also used in traditional remedies like tonics and syrups to treat various health conditions.
